title: Northern Illinois Freeze-Thaw Climate Data (NOAA NCEI)
content: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information historical data for Lake County, IL: Average annual freeze-thaw cycles: 70-85 (measured as days where temperature crosses 32°F). Peak freeze-thaw months: November (8-12 cycles), December (10-15), January (12-18), February (10-16), March (10-14). Average winter precipitation: 8.5 inches (liquid equivalent). Average annual snowfall: 36-42 inches. Road salt application threshold: 32°F and falling or when black ice risk is elevated. De-icing chemical degradation: NaCl weakens asphalt binder at roughly 3x the rate of plain water. Spring pothole surge typically peaks in late March/early April when deep frost heaves thaw. Source: NOAA NCEI Climate Normals 1991-2020, Station USW00094846 (Chicago O'Hare).
